feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP IPS: Wrong value of schemas attribute
entry.content:
Helo Experts,

When I create the source system as IAS and the target system as SAC, provisioning to SAC works fine initially. However, when I make some changes or copy the code from the SAP documentation for SCIM API 2 and  a few errors appear - "Duplicated target paths warning and I try to resolve the errors, but I am unable to fix them.

Then, when I revert to the original transformation code (the one that worked at the beginning), I reinsert it, and provisioning stops working.

I get this error:

Caused by: Could not process successfully all entities from system with name: Source IAS

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant.cloud.sap/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema. Wrong value of schemas attribute"},

content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Then nothing works anymore, and I have to delete the systems and create new ones.

Please help me understand how to avoid or fix such errors."

What am I doing wrong?

Many Thanks

entry.title: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Directory - Differences
entry.content:
Hello All,

What is the difference between SAP Identity Authentication and Local Identity Directory in SAP IPS/IAS ?

In both cases, users are stored in SAP IAS, but I don't understand the difference and when each solution should be used.

Thank you very much!

 

BEst Regards

entry.title: SAP Enable Now und IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured IAS as a proxy and am provisioning users from Azure to IAS and then to SAP Enable Now Azure->IAS->. How can roles be automatically assigned to provisioned users in Enable Now ?

Is there something similar to groups/teams in SAP Enable Now, like in SAC, that can be granted access via a role?

Unfortunately, I can't find any information on this.

Many Thanks for any help!

entry.title: Applications, Single and Composite roles are not displayed in IAG Access request
entry.content:
Hi All,

While trying to raise a Access request in IAG, no single role, composite role is appearing to be selected. Also, if a user has a application and want to add more roles for the same application, then the same does  not appear . 

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: IPS: Email and Telephone Not Provisioned to Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Why are the email and phone number attributes not being provisioned  to Enable Now using the default transformation code?

I am provisioning users with SAP IPS  from Azure to IAS and then to Enable Now.

Many Thanks

entry.title: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned users from IAS to SAC.

However, when I change a user in IAS (e.g., updating the last name) and run the Read job, the changes are not provisioned to the target system (SAC).

There is no error, but the users are read, and no update is performed in the target system.

What should I pay attention to and what needs to be configured to ensure that only the user changes are successfully provisioned?

 

Many Thanks

Best regard

entry.title: SAP Commerce cloud integration with SAP IAG as SCIM system
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP Commerce cloud integration with SAP IAG as SCIM system .

IN Swagger connectivity and details are about to pull.

In IAG, getting repository synch fail with 500 error.

Please suggest.

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

entry.title: Best Practices for Managing Users and Groups in Azure and IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, we have the following scenario implemented: We have Azure as the IdP → IAS as a proxy → Cloud Apps. The groups are created in Azure, and users are assigned to those groups in Azure. These groups with their users are then provisioned to SAP IAS. From SAP IAS, the users/groups, such as SAC_Group, are provisioned to the SAC app, or the group ENABLE_Group is provisioned to Enable Now.

Since we, as the SAP team, do not have access to the Azure tenant, I am asking if there is a better approach or any best practices for managing users/groups.

Could we provision only users from Azure and create the groups directly in IAS, then manually assign users to these groups und then provision to Cloud Apps? Are there any disadvantages to this approach?

What are your experiences? How do you handle this in your environment? What are the best practices: Should users/groups be managed in Azure, or should groups be managed directly in IAS?

Which approach is better: Managing users/groups in Azure or provisioning users to IAS and managing the groups there?

Thank you so much!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The following problem:

As far as I know, the UPN is typically set as an email address.

In my scenario, users are provisioned from Azure -> IAS -> cloud applications.

However, when provisioning users from IAS  (for example) to Enable Now, the usernames are created as email addresses.

 

 

How can I avoid this?

For example, in Entra ID Source System, the User Name is defined in the Default Code as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},

So, this means that the UPN (Azure) becomes the Login Name ( in IAS).

For example, if I map the employee ID to the username in the IPS transformation, the user is created in Enable Now with the employee ID (which would be ideal for us) as the username, but the users cannot authenticate in Azure with the employee ID.

Is there a way to change the UPN?

Or is there another solution to ensure that the usernames in cloud applications are not created as email addresses, while users can still authenticate in Azure?

Thank you very much !!!

Best regards

entry.title: SAP IAG TandD application plan
entry.content:
Hi All,

Please let know the meaning of TandD application plan for SAP IAG

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Error in group provisioning to commerce cloud
entry.content:
Hello All,

Error occurring in Commerce cloud group provisioning .

User able to create bit fails in group provisioning.

Please suggest

Thanks

entry.title: User Attributes Not Transferred to SAP BTP from Azure AD via IAS with OIDC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured SAP IAS as a proxy and Azure as IdP, with OIDC as protocol between Azure and IAS. I am trying to log  in BAS. but I have encountered an issue- Access denied.  The User is created in SAP BTP but the user name are not transferred correctly und first und last name are not transferred at all to SAP BTP:

Configuration in IAS:

  • Subject Name Identifier: (Corporate Identity Provider) - NameID

     

  • Attributes:

     

     

Configuration in Azure:

  • The necessary claims such as are available in Azure AD.

Where could the issue be in the configuration?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards.

entry.title: IPS Error with SCIM 2
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am provisioning users from Azure to IAS, which works well so far. After that, I provision the users to SAC, but I encounter the following error:

user:34asa-34df-4434f-435f-5fgfgsdfgsfdgsdg,

system=Target - SAC

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://sac-tenant.com/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"mutability","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema."},

 content={"active":true,"displayName":"sacfirst, saclast","emails":[{"primary":true,"value":"sacfirst.saclast@domain.com"}],"groups":[{"value":"438b6f2a-5ec4-4d93-b9f6-13cb4ee9502d"}],"name":{"familyName":"saclast","givenName":"sacfirst"},"schemas":["urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"],"urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters":{"idpUserId":"sacfirst.saclast@domain.com"},"userName":"sacfirst.saclast@domain.com"}

When I provision the users to IAS, the login name is set as the email address in IAS.

After that, the users are provisioned from IAS to SAC, and the error mentioned above occurs. We found that the error occurs because the login name from IAS (the email address) is transferred to the "USER ID" field in SAC. However, SAC does not allow the "USER ID" to be an email address.

This error happens with  SAP Analytics Cloud SCIM API version 2:

IAS Properties - sac.api.version=2, csrf.token.path=/api/v1/scim2/Users?count=1.

I copied the transformation code from the SAP help page for the target system - SAC.

How can this issue be resolved?

Thank you for your time and assistance.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Azure, IAS, and Cloud Apps Authentication
entry.content:
Hi All, Hi @Matthew_Shaw  

@Matthew_Shaw thank you so much for your help!

Now a clarification question: We have Azure as IdP, IAS as a proxy, and cloud apps like SAC configured. We provision users from Azure to IAS and then to SAC.

Ideally, we would like to log in to the apps  (like SAC) using an employee number (e.g., 283268).

I can of course provision the employee number (283268) from Azure to IAS in the "Login Name" field and then to SAP SAC in the "USER ID". However, when a user tries to log in to SAC, they cannot log in with the employee number (283268). This user is not found in Azure. If the user logs in with the email address, the login works. What am I doing wrong here?

Question 1: Is it possible to authenticate in cloud apps (such as SAC) using an employee number from Azure (since users are redirected to Azure for authentication)?

Question 2: In Azure, the UPN is set as the email address, e.g., firstname.lastname@mail.com. If I form the username "f.lastname" from firstname.lastname@mail.com and transfer it from Azure -> IAS -> SAC, can the user log in with "f.lastname"? Will this user be found in Azure with "f.lastname"? What is the best attribute from Azure to provision to the USER ID for authentication?

Thank you again. Unfortunately, I am stuck here and need help.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Managing User Roles Directly in Azure
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
We use Azure as an IdP, SAP Cloud Identity Services as a proxy, and various cloud apps. Is there a way to grant access to users who are accessing a cloud app directly in Azure, i.e., to assign roles (what a user can do within the cloud app)? In other words, to assign roles to the users in Azure and not in the cloud app? And is this even recommended?

Thanks you very much.

 

Best regards

entry.title: Self-registration workflow in IAS
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

is there a way to set up a self-registration workflow in SAP IAS where users can submit a request for account creation, when Azure is used as IdP ?

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: Creation custom attribute cloud identity service not visible
entry.content: HI! I am trying to create a custom attribute in my cloud identity service, and then see in the login details and use it in the SCIM api request.  However, I am not getting positive results. Even after following the guide 'https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-user-attributes-sent-to-application,' once the custom field is created, it is not visible among the attributes of old and new users.  How can I do this? thanks

entry.title: Subject Name Identifiier in IAS
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a question regarding the Subject Name Identifier in IAS.

I’ve been experimenting in my test environment and here's what I did:

  • In Azure, I created a Claim employee_id=user.employee_id.
  • In IAS, I set the Subject Name Identifier to Corporate Identity Provider=employee_id.
  • The user was created in SAC/BTP with the User Name being the employee_id (User Name filed = 131414)

Then I changed the employee_id in Azure, but the user was still able to log in.

Only when I removed the email=user.email claim in Azure (ODIC), the user could no longer log in to BTP.

I thought the SNI was the main factor for identifying the user in the cloud app.

So, for example, if the user with SNI=234324 is sent to BTP, and only the user with SNI (User Name in Cloud App) 1111 exists in BTP, the user should no longer be able to log in – but this is not the case. It seems that, even though the setting in IAS - Subject Name Identifier=employee_id , the user is identified/found by the email address in BTP

Why is that? Or did I misunderstand the purpose of the Subject Name Identifier?

Do I understand it correctly that the Subject Name Identifier is always equal to the User Name in a cloud app (SNI = User Name in SAC)

Thank you very much.

Best Regards

entry.title: IPS Delta Read
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have defined the following properties in the Source System (Azure): ips.full.read.force.count=3.

In the Target System (Identity Authentication), I have defined this property: ips.delete.existedbefore.entities=true.

Then, I deleted a group in IAS. I thought that when the READ job is executed 3 times (which would be Delta Read), a Full Read would be triggered on the 4th run, and the deleted group would be re-provisioned from the Source System to the Target System.

However, this is not happening.

What am I doing wrong?

How can I schedule the Delta Read job? I cannot find the ips.delta.read=enebaled parameter in either the Source or the Target system.

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: OpenID for SAC
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is it possible to configure OpenID Connect for user authentication  (SSO)  between SAC and IAS, when IAS is configured as a proxy ?

Or is SAML 2.0 the only supported and recommended method?

Thank you.
